key_findings: M2 half-turn rule falsified; multi-island tempo structure is the better interpretation.
v1.5 spatial nucleation/domain phenomena were largely eaten by a classical CA ablation.
In v2, anti-phase domain wall is a phase-field effect, not quantum evidence; classical wave reproduces it.
Adjacent-pair negativity remains the strongest quantum witness in the lattice core.
ZZ attraction preserves already-adjacent clusters but does not reliably merge separated excitations in a closed unitary system.
History attraction follows old terrain but tends to delocalize the droplet.
History repulsion avoids old terrain and tends to compact the droplet.
Repel compaction is largely classical self-avoidance, but transport/path selection depends on quantum phase.
Edge memory is more active than bulk memory in the current core.
current_hypothesis: self-avoiding edge-memory terrain plus quantum phase routing is the next nontrivial target
next_experiment: 2D phase-routed self-avoiding quantum droplet with classical probability ablation
